comment_text.sn1.40:2.1: An apt description of the daughter of the thunderstorm.

root_text.sn1.40:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn1.40:1.3: Atha kho cūḷakokanadā pajjunnassa dhītā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ mahāvanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn1.40:1.4: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā devatā cūḷakokanadā pajjunnassa dhītā bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn1.40:0.2: 4. The Host of a Hundred Felicities
translation_text.sn1.40:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn1.40:1.3: Then, late at night, the glorious Kokanadā the Younger, Pajjunna’s daughter, lighting up the entire Great Wood, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side,
translation_text.sn1.40:1.4: and recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.40:2.1: “She came here, beautiful as a flash of lightning,
translation_text.sn1.40:2.3: Revering the Buddha and the teaching,
translation_text.sn1.40:2.4: she spoke these verses full of meaning.
translation_text.sn1.40:3.2: I could analyze it in many different ways.
translation_text.sn1.40:3.3: However, I will state the meaning in brief
translation_text.sn1.40:3.4: as far as I have learned it by heart.
translation_text.sn1.40:4.1: You should never do anything bad
translation_text.sn1.40:4.2: by speech or mind or body in all the world.
translation_text.sn1.40:4.3: Having given up sensual pleasures,  <j>mindful and aware,
translation_text.sn1.40:4.4: you shouldn’t keep doing  <j>what’s painful and pointless.”

reference_text.sn1.40:1.1: cck15.40, csp1ed12.28, csp2ed12.28, ms12S1_231, msdiv40, pts-vp-pli2ed1.63, sya15.42
